name: CASE Generated Balanced Synthetic EFL Essay Samples Across Score Ranges
statement: Yoo and colleagues introduced CASE, a corruption-based augmentation strategy that generated 40,185 synthetic EFL essay samples with balanced score ranges for rubric-based automated essay scoring.
population_or_context: EFL undergraduate writing scored on content, organization, and language dimensions.
quantitative_note: DREsS_CASE has the same number of samples per score, and adding standardized plus CASE data improved the reported baseline by 45.44%.
evaluation_method: Rubric-based AES QWK on real DREsS_New validation/test essays and synthetic quality checks comparing CASE with gpt-4o generated essays.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: The paper reports scarce low-score samples and lack of usable rubric-based AES datasets.
synthetic_data_research_role: Synthetic essays served as training augmentation and score-distribution balancing for rubric-based scorer development.
reported_metrics: 40.1K CASE samples; 45.44% baseline improvement; CASE synthetic-label QWK 0.661 versus gpt-4o average 0.225.
limitations: CASE relies on corruption of well-written essays.
The method may not cover all authentic EFL learner writing patterns.
